title: Innovative Cold Chain Policies to Limit Salmonella Growth in Perishable Foods
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illness     globally  ,   with     perishable     foods     such   as   meat  ,   dairy  ,   and     fresh     produce     representing     significant     transmission     routes  .   Temperature     abuse     during     storage     and     transport     allows     Salmonella     to     multiply     rapidly  ,   posing     a     serious     public     health     threat  .   Strengthening     cold     chain     management     is     essential     to     break     this     cycle  .   Innovative     policies  —  combining   real-  time     temperature     monitoring  , digital   traceability     systems  ,   and     incentives     for     compliance  —  can     help     ensure     that     perishable     foods     remain     within     safe     temperature     ranges     from     production     to     consumption  . By   aligning     regulatory     frameworks     with     technological     solutions  ,   governments     can     minimize     the     incidence     of     Salmonella     outbreaks  ,   reduce     economic     losses  ,   and     strengthen     consumer     confidence   in   food     safety     systems  .
